original_hypothesis.title: Owner Can Drain All TITN From MergeTgt Via Unrestricted withdraw() Including User-Allocated Amounts
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The withdraw() function allows the owner to transfer any amount of any ERC20 token from the MergeTgt contract, including the TITN allocated to users via claimableTitnPerUser. There is no check that the withdrawal preserves solvency (titn.balanceOf >= totalTitnClaimable). The owner can drain all TITN, permanently blocking all claimTitn and withdrawRemainingTitn calls which rely on safeTransfer that would revert with insufficient balance. This also corrupts the withdrawRemainingTitn snapshot if called before the 360-day mark.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The withdraw function has no restrictions on token type or amount. It accepts TITN as the token parameter and can transfer user-allocated funds to the owner. The solvency invariant (INV-ECON-001) is directly violated.

proof_of_signal.pattern: withdraw(IERC20 token, uint256 amount) performs safeTransfer to owner() with no validation that token != titn or that amount preserves solvency

preconditions: Owner account is compromised or acts maliciously

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: withdraw accepts titn as token parameter with no solvency check; same function used for legitimate TGT recovery and malicious TITN extraction
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires owner action - this IS the admin rug pull vector

operational_questions: Is the owner a multisig or EOA?
Are there any off-chain safeguards monitoring withdraw calls?
Is ownership transferred to a governance contract post-deployment?

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Owner (or compromised owner key) calls withdraw(titn, titn.balanceOf(address(this)))
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: All TITN transferred to owner address
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: All user claimTitn and withdrawRemainingTitn calls revert due to insufficient balance

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Withdraw events for TITN token from MergeTgt
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Owner withdraw followed by user claim reverts

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
